criteria.label: Write L(θ) from a PMF/PDF with data fixed and state the probability vs likelihood roles.
criteria.example: Given y=1 from Bernoulli(p), write L(p)=p; explain that f(y|p) with y fixed is likelihood, while f(y|p) with p fixed over y∈{0,1} is probability.

criteria.label: Build joint log-likelihood for IID observations and identify the MLE for a simple model.
criteria.example: For 8 heads in 10 flips, write L(p)=p^8(1−p)^2 and ℓ(p)=8 log p + 2 log(1−p); conclude MLE p̂=0.8.

criteria.label: Connect negative log-likelihood to a standard ML loss on a numeric example.
criteria.example: For y=1, p=0.9, compute NLL −log(0.9) ≈ 0.105; for y=0, p=0.9, compute −log(1−0.9) ≈ 2.303 and explain which prediction fits the label better.

criteria.label: Place likelihood correctly in Bayes' theorem and contrast with MLE.
criteria.example: Write posterior ∝ prior × L(θ) for coin bias p after 7/10 heads; note MLE uses only L(p) giving p̂=0.7, while Beta(1,1) prior yields a Beta posterior, not P(p=0.7|data)=1.

criteria.label: Implement and compare log-likelihood values in code for at least two candidate θ.
criteria.example: In Python, for Poisson counts [3,5,4,6], compute `poisson.logpmf(y, mu=4.5).sum()` vs `mu=2.0` and report which λ has higher ℓ(λ).
